prose: The evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis for simvastatin resolves to negative, and the reasoning rests squarely on curated mechanism: there is no curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and no registration as a neuro-AOP stressor [E1], so the engine finds no diagnostic basis on which to flag this compound. The bioactivity profile is deliberately not decisive here—17 mechanistic assay hits including 2 mitochondrial signals [E2] represent exactly the kind of imposter corroboration that, absent curated mechanistic anchoring, does not support a positive call. Real-world pharmacovigilance is concordant with this restraint: across 261,343 FAERS reports no parkinsonism signal emerges (ROR 1.07, not elevated) [E3]. Brain penetrance [E4] confirms plausible CNS exposure but, without a curated mechanistic target, cannot discriminate risk and adds nothing to the call. This is an adversarial-negative case: bioactive yet correctly unflagged, because the engine reasons on curated mechanism, not assay activity alone.

citations.detail: No curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and not a registered neuro-AOP stressor.
Bioactive: 17 mechanistic assay hits (incl. 2 mitochondrial). This is the imposter signal the engine correctly does NOT act on.
Zero parkinsonism signal across 261343 FAERS reports (ROR 1.07, not elevated).
Brain-penetrant, but penetrance is not a discriminator here.
